iotevents.alarmmodel.CfnAbout: Represents an alarm model to monitor an AWS IoT Events input attribute. You can use the alarm to get notified when the value is outside a specified range. For more information, see [Create an alarm model](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/iotevents/latest/developerguide/create-alarms.html) in the * AWS IoT Events Developer Guide*.

iotevents.alarmmodel.AlarmCapabilities.Description: Contains the configuration information of alarm state changes.

iotevents.alarmmodel.AlarmEventActions.Description: Contains information about one or more alarm actions.

iotevents.alarmmodel.AlarmModelDescripti.Description: The description of the alarm model.

iotevents.alarmmodel.AlarmModelName.Description: The name of the alarm model.

iotevents.alarmmodel.AlarmRule.Description: Defines when your alarm is invoked.

iotevents.alarmmodel.Key.Description: An input attribute used as a key to create an alarm. AWS IoT Events routes [inputs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/iotevents/latest/apireference/API_Input.html) associated with this key to the alarm.

iotevents.alarmmodel.RoleArn.Description: The ARN of the IAM role that allows the alarm to perform actions and access AWS resources. For more information, see [Amazon Resource Names (ARNs)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) in the * AWS General Reference*.

iotevents.alarmmodel.Severity.Description: A non-negative integer that reflects the severity level of the alarm.

iotevents.alarmmodel.Tags.Description: A list of key-value pairs that contain metadata for the alarm model. The tags help you manage the alarm model. For more information, see [Tagging your AWS IoT Events resources](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/iotevents/latest/developerguide/tagging-iotevents.html) in the * AWS IoT Events Developer Guide*.  You can create up to 50 tags for one alarm model.
